parentQuestion: A trauma patient arrives with multiple injuries affecting skin, muscle, bone, an...

scaffolds.q: The four primary tissue types are epithelial, connective, muscle, and nervous.
scaffolds.explain: Every tissue in your body falls into one of four categories: EPITHELIAL (covers, lines, secretes), CONNECTIVE (supports, connects, transports), MUSCLE (contracts, moves), NERVOUS (senses, signals). That's it. Four fundamental building blocks. Proceed with me:.

scaffolds.q: Most body regions contain multiple tissue types working together.
scaffolds.explain: Body regions and organs are almost never made of just ONE tissue type. They're COMPOSITE structures containing multiple tissue types collaborating for function. Accept this reality:into this: cooperation between tissue types is what enables complex function.
